message.content:
Let $v$ be the speed of the boat in still water, and let $c$ be the speed of the current.
The distance between the two ports along the river is 60 km.
The time taken for the round trip is 3 hours and 40 minutes, which is $3 + \frac{40}{60} = 3 + \frac{2}{3} = \frac{9+2}{3} = \frac{11}{3}$ hours.
The distance for the round trip is $2 \times 60 = 120$ km.
When the boat travels against the current, the distance covered is $60$ km. The time taken is $\frac{60}{v}$.
When the boat travels with the current, the distance covered is $60$ km. The time taken is $\frac{60}{v+3}$.
The total time for the round trip is $\frac{11}{3}$ hours.
So we have
$$ \frac{60}{v} + \frac{60}{v+3} = \frac{11}{3} $$
Multiplying by $3v(v+3)$ gives
$$ 3(60)(v+3) + 3(60)v = \frac{11}{3} \cdot 3v(v+3) $$
$$ 180(v+3) + 180v = 1
